The LTL property states that globally, if time constraints are present, then the robot explains whether the plan changes or not. This captures the essence of the prompt, which is to address the impact of time constraints on the robot's plan.

The property is a conditional statement, checking if time constraints influence a path change.

This LTL property states that globally, if there are time constraints, then the robot should explain the situation if the plan changes. The formula ensures that whenever time constraints are present, the robot is obligated to provide an explanation about plan changes.

This LTL property states that globally, if time constraints are present, then eventually the robot must explain why its plan is changing, either immediately or for a duration within a defined window.

The property uses the 'G' (Globally) and '->' (implication) operators to capture this behavior. The boolean expressions define the conditions related to time constraints and plan changes.

explanation: This LTL property states that globally, if time constraints are present and potentially influence the plan or actions of the robot (bool_exp1), and there is a change in plan (bool_exp2), then it is always the case that the robot provides an explanation for those time constraints changing the plan (bool_exp3), or the explanation is provided immediately in the next state (X bool_exp3).
The LTL property states that globally, if time constraints are present, then the robot should explain the path it is currently taking, or should explain the path it is planning to take.
This LTL property states that globally, if time constraints are present, then eventually the robot's plan will change. This is a direct translation of the prompt.

This LTL property states that globally, if time constraints are present, then it should always be explained what the time constraints change about the plan. In other words, whenever there are time constraints, the system should provide an explanation that includes changes to the plan.

This LTL property checks that globally, whenever time constraints are present and influence the plan, the robot is expected to explain the path it is currently taking, and the system will provide information about the path it is currently taking. The LTL uses the 'G' (Globally) operator to ensure this happens throughout the entire execution. The '->' (implication) ensures that if 'timeConstraints' is true, then 'explainCurrentPath' is true, and 'returnCurrentPath' is true. This property is designed to ensure the system provides an explanation of changes when time constraints are a factor.

This LTL property states that globally, if time constraints are present (bool_exp1), then it should provide a detailed explanation (bool_exp3). The property also states that if time constraints are present, then it will provide a detailed explanation of why the plan is changing, as this is part of a plan change. The property should explain the impact of time constraints which change plan.

This LTL property states that globally, if there are time constraints, then the robot should explain if time constraints change the plan. This is captured by the G (globally) operator, ensuring that this holds true at every time step. The implication operator -> links the time constraints with the explanation being given if changes are happening.
This LTL property states that globally, if time constraints are present, then the robot should explain alternatives.

The LTL property ensures that globally, if time constraints are present, then it will always explain why the plan is changing. This captures the core requirement of explaining the impact of time constraints.
This LTL property states that globally, if time constraints are present, then it must be explained if time constraints change the plan. We are using a template that encodes the implication.

This LTL property states that globally, if there are time constraints, then it should globally be the case that the robot explains the path it is currently taking or the path it is planning to take, or the robot explains the path it intends to take, for the duration of N_DURATION time steps. This property ensures that the robot provides an explanation of its path when time constraints are in effect.

This LTL property states that globally, if there are time constraints (`timeConstraints`), then it will be the case that, the robot explains why the plan is changing or the plan is changing (`changePlan`), or the robot explains why the plan is changing or the plan is changing (`changePlan`). The boolean expressions are simple atomic propositions.

This LTL property states that if time constraints are present, then eventually the plan is changed.  The property uses the 'G' (Globally) operator to ensure that the implication holds true at all times and that if the time constraints are present, the plan will eventually change.
